Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi, Andreas Oct 7th, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Progress report •Important! : 2nd batch experiment arrangement •Saun: Start in mid-Nov, will get ﬁnished before her vacation. •She would like to double-check the size of the slides with us -> •Network analysis for immune interaction events •A subgroup of HRP patients show few immune interaction events spatially, along with poor progression •Ziqi performed downstream analysis on deconvoluted bulk RNA-seq with PRISM •External validation for cellular proportion differences HRD vs HRP •Andreas: CNA calling result & improvement •Patient CNA clusters + SPACEstat metrics

A subgroup of HRP patients show few immune interaction events spatially, along with poor prognosis
+
•Define immune interaction events •Network 1: including B, CD4T, CD8T, NK, Treg. •Network 2: including DC, Macrophages •Find the overlapping components from 1 & 2, merge them into a polygon “immune hub” -> immune interaction events. •Parameters: •Longest edge when forming the network: 35 µm •Distance between network components: <10 µm •Minimum #cell: 30 •From hub we have info: •Cellular proportion in each hub •Location of the hub centroid -> we are using this today! •Size of the hub (#cell included)Comment: Consider 1) which cell types are we interested in and 2) how can we tune the parameters

A subgroup of HRP patients show few immune interaction events spatially, along with poor prognosis•Define immune interaction events •Network 1: including B, CD4T, CD8T, NK, Treg. •Network 2: including DC, Macrophages •Find the overlapping components from 1 & 2, merge them into a polygon “immune hub” -> immune interaction events. •Parameters: •Longest edge when forming the network: 35 µm •Distance between network components: <10 µm •Minimum #cell: 30 •From hub we have info: •Cellular proportion in each hub •Location of the hub centroid -> we are using this today! •Size of the hub (#cell included)To study the spatial distribution of the immune interaction events quantitatively. We get inspired by the ecological methods. 
L(r) — the Ripley’s L function (a variance-stabilized form of Ripley’s K-function), g(r) — the pair correlation function (PCF), describing local spatial density at distance r.

Are those features relevant with PFS/OS? 
🤔

Downstream analysis on deconvoluted bulk RNA-seq•edgeR (DGE, HRD vs HRP) + GO analysis •Test run: with HRD & HRP groups. •Next step: relabel the patients based on CNA status or spatial features! -> And rerun the analysis

External validation for cellular proportions between HRD & HRPGoal: validate the CD4T & CD8T cellular% differences found from tCycIF 
•TCGA bulk RNA-seq, deconcoluted with CIBERSORT, choose p-value <0.05 •41 HRD, 38 HRP left •scRNAseq dataset from Sciset •13 HRD, 14 HRP
🤔

CNA calling result•(Update from Andreas) •Put a complex heatmap here

CNA calling result•Identiﬁed 12 CCNE, 18 MYC, and 7 MECOM gene ampliﬁcations. •These targets were selected due to their relevance as known COSMIC genes and their distribution across different chromosomes, making them suitable candidates for SPACE analysis. •Pipeline Improvements and Next Steps •Plan to run copy number signatures through GISTIC2 to identify amplification peaks and select genes near these peaks that are also annotated in COSMIC. •A Docker environment was required for this analysis; however, current limitations suggest it may not support the necessary amount of reference data. •May request assistance from the Data Team to host or download the required reference data on the server. •These steps are expected to increase the usability of data for other projects and to improve validation of selected genes. •Integration of RNA-seq data will further validate identified amplifications. •Discussed with Sara, who has a normalised gene expression matrix and established RNA-seq pipelines ( used for Ada). These can be readily adapted for this analysis. •The plan is to correlate gene amplifications with RNA expression levels: •Samples showing gene amplification should also exhibit high expression levels. •True amplifications are expected to fall within the highest quartile of expression on the gene level.

amples showing gene amplification should also exhibit high expression levels. •True amplifications are expected to fall within the highest quartile of expression on the gene level. •Combining GISTIC, COSMIC, and RNA-seq validation will strengthen the evidence that the detected ampliﬁcations are biologically meaningful and represent genuine CNAs.

CNAs are associated with opposite trends of spatial features in HRP patients
The comparison is performed between HRP - 1 vs HRP - 0. The unknown patients are ignored.

Next meeting? Thanks for listening!

Supplement!
